title: India supply-chain exposure to Hormuz disruption — primary-source curation
purpose: Five most-exposed Indian sectors with citation-backed exposure values. Each row is a fact PLUS its public URL. Sectors below 'commercial LPG' tier (e.g. household plastics, regional logistics) are deliberately omitted; we trade row count for citation quality.
curation_rule: Every numeric value carries source_type ∈ {primary, secondary, model_estimate}. No row published without a working URL.

exposure_facts.claim: India imports approximately 60% of its LPG demand.
exposure_facts.publisher: Petroleum Planning & Analysis Cell (PPAC), MoPNG, Government of India
exposure_facts.title: Snapshot of India's Oil & Gas Data — LPG section

exposure_facts.claim: Saudi Arabia, UAE and Qatar collectively supply ~70% of India's imported LPG.
exposure_facts.title: PPAC Ready Reckoner — LPG imports source country

sectors.first_symptom_when_hormuz_hits: Commercial 19 kg cylinders tighten first; refilling delays at hotels, restaurants, small industry. Government typically protects domestic 14.2 kg cylinders during shortages.

first_symptom_evidence.claim: During the 2022 commodity stress, the Ministry of Petroleum directed prioritised domestic LPG allocation over commercial.
first_symptom_evidence.publisher: Press Information Bureau (PIB), Government of India
first_symptom_evidence.title: MoPNG advisory on LPG priority allocation, June 2022

first_symptom_evidence.claim: Goa hoteliers and Maharashtra commercial users reported commercial LPG tightness in mid-2025 during Gulf shipping disruption — domestic protected, commercial squeezed.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Goa hotels report commercial LPG shortage — PTI

first_symptom_evidence.claim: Maharashtra commercial LPG supply tightening reported amid rerouting.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Maharashtra commercial LPG supply tightens

exposure_facts.claim: India urea production is gas-feedstock-based; ~80% of domestic urea capacity uses natural gas (predominantly imported LNG) as feedstock.
exposure_facts.publisher: Department of Fertilizers, Government of India
exposure_facts.title: Annual Report 2023-24

exposure_facts.claim: Qatar supplied ~37% of India's LNG imports in 2023-24, the largest single source; this LNG transits Hormuz.
exposure_facts.title: Natural Gas section, PPAC Snapshot

sectors.first_symptom_when_hormuz_hits: Urea production rates squeeze if LNG is rationed; imported urea + DAP prices spike on global markets. Kharif/Rabi sowing input costs rise.

first_symptom_evidence.claim: Hormuz / LNG disruption creates measurable risk to ammonia and downstream urea production globally.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Hormuz risk and global ammonia/urea markets

first_symptom_evidence.claim: India urea import volumes increased ~25% during 2022 European gas crisis; Hormuz disruption would tighten further.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Urea import statistics dashboard

sectors.sector_name: Refining & transport fuels (diesel, petrol, ATF, naphtha)

exposure_facts.claim: India imports approximately 87% of its crude oil consumption.
exposure_facts.title: Crude Oil Imports Statistics

exposure_facts.claim: Iraq, Saudi Arabia, and UAE together supply approximately 33-40% of India's crude basket; majority transits Hormuz.
exposure_facts.title: Indian Petroleum & Natural Gas Statistics 2023-24

sectors.first_symptom_when_hormuz_hits: Crude landed cost rises within days; OMC retail diesel/petrol prices follow with regulatory lag. ATF (jet fuel) deregulated, so airline fares respond fastest.

first_symptom_evidence.claim: ATF accounts for ~40-45% of Indian airline operating costs and is repriced fortnightly.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Indian carrier fuel cost share

exposure_facts.claim: India's largest petchem clusters (Reliance Jamnagar, IOCL Panipat, GAIL Pata, HPCL/HMEL Bathinda) draw heavily on imported naphtha + LPG feedstock with significant Gulf origin.
exposure_facts.title: Refinery throughput and feedstock disclosures

sectors.first_symptom_when_hormuz_hits: Plastics, packaging, polymer prices rise within 2-4 weeks. FMCG cost-of-goods inflation follows.

first_symptom_evidence.claim: Polymer (HDPE, PP, PVC) global prices historically track LPG/naphtha at 30-60 day lag.
first_symptom_evidence.title: Polymer-feedstock price linkage

exposure_facts.claim: War-risk insurance premia for vessels transiting Hormuz historically spike 10–20× during major escalations (e.g. 2019 tanker incidents).
exposure_facts.publisher: Lloyd's List / IUMI (International Union of Marine Insurance)
exposure_facts.title: Hormuz war-risk premium volatility

exposure_facts.claim: Indian-flagged tankers and chartered vessels at Mundra, Kandla, Sikka regularly transit Hormuz; insurance and freight surcharges pass through to landed cost of crude/LPG/LNG.
exposure_facts.title: INSA freight cost reports

sectors.first_symptom_when_hormuz_hits: Insurance war-risk premiums double-digit overnight; charter rates rise within a week; landed crude/LPG/LNG cost rises 4-12% within first month.

explicitly_omitted_sectors.reason: Aviation impact is a downstream effect of ATF prices (already captured in Sector 3). Listing as a separate row would double-count.
Government-prioritized; primary impact occurs only in prolonged disruptions >60 days. We list commercial LPG as Sector 1 because the immediate stress lands there.
India power mix is ~70% coal + ~10% renewables + ~6% gas; Hormuz LNG disruption affects power only marginally.
